label: Latin American History

sources.title: Reform and Revolution in Latin America

app_prompts: Colonial Latin America timeline: user picks a colonial institution (encomienda, mita, hacienda) and traces how it shaped labor, race, and land — from Spanish conquest through independence.
Latin American revolution explorer: user picks a 20th-century revolution (Mexico, Cuba, Nicaragua, El Salvador) and compares the causes, the outcome, and the US response.
Land reform case study: user picks a country and era — Mexico 1917, Guatemala 1952, Chile 1970 — and traces the agrarian reform: who got land, who resisted, what the US did.
Indigenous sovereignty timeline: user picks a region and traces indigenous resistance to colonial and postcolonial states — from the encomienda system to the Zapatista movement.
Historical memory quiz: user reads a description of a historical event (La Matanza, Guatemalan genocide, dirty wars) and traces how it was suppressed, remembered, and eventually recognized.
